author: @tomaszmaciag6484
text:
Robert, you use 304 stainless steel mesh but this steel corrodes in acid.
Maybe instead of this mesh choose a 316 stainless steel mesh, this type of steel is resistant to strong acids and alkalis.
it would be less work with laminating and protecting the mesh against electrolyte.
What do you think ???

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: 316 is better but we are encapsulating so in theory we could use aluminium

author: @Karabetter
text:
Great video! Welding is a nice way to handle stainless steel battery connection tabs. :)
Question: After seeing your previous "battery cell" construction, and now comparing to the "capacitor cell"... you reused the *separator pouch* that came in the battery. But what do you use as the *separator* in the capacitor? 
(I wouldn't think paper towel would be stable enough for a reasonable life expectancy.)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: paper is fine mate

author: @tomaszmaciag6484
text: Robert, is a thinner steel grid covered with plastic is it better to use graphite foil ??

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: yes the graphic will soak up the electrolyte and become fragile quite quickly

replies.author: @tomaszmaciag6484
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering OK - thanks for the clarification !

author: @davidmayhall6567
text: How was the twizzy battery's made was they pouch battery's or did you do the same as here?   Have you made a solid state Battrey yet?  What is the conductive plastic? Did you make that? I need two battery's 12-14 volts 35 Amp hours or more to operate my donated wheelchair. I cannot operate my manual chair up hills from a spinal injury. I was thinking of a solid type Battrey /capacitor to operate my wheelchair, and made to fit the Battrey cavity in my wheelchair

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: this is all experimental stuff mate - it isn't going to replace you battery anytime soon

replies.author: @samuel5823yo
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering we should just send money to you to experiment for us🤣🤣🤣

author: @davidmayhall6567
text: I was wondering how you seal the Battrey back up? Looks quite messy like it will leak all over and ruin what the Battrey is in,  My wheelchair battery's  are Dead and I need new ones I have been thinking about fixing them but I don't want a Mess all over battery's leaking on my floor all over. Would  a pouch type system be better than using that old Battrey case and perhaps melting strips of plastic pouch type material on each side of the tabs at the base of the strip so the strips can be melted together  then welded at the tops so a pouch type container can be made by laying plastic over the cells and welding the edges closed? I watched your vacuum sealer video where pouches was made, perhaps smaller amount of electrolyte saturate Matt material and build sandwich type Battrey and seal together in pouches. I was wondering about cells gasskng and pouches expanding perhaps pouches can fit into old Battrey case and tabs are insulated with plastic on each side last inch of tabs are Bare so they can be welded together into a pile 2volts per cell is needed, actually my wheelchair battery's charge to 14 volts, they are dead boiled dry no electrolyte left. I refilled them with water and tryed to charge them they won't take a charge. How can I rejuvenate my old battery's without making a mess of them? Can I add new acid and charge them?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: seriously mate - this is just a prototype - I would probably glue the top on it wouldn't be that difficult and it would be done prior to electrolyte fill so it wouldn't be that messy - but this is to encourage others to experiment not to present a completed battery

author: @4gauge10
text: Looks good,I'm looking forward to seeing how it specs out.

replies.author: @azurealism
replies.text: Ditto's - we have a golf cart, and batteries are horrifically unreliable despite how expensive they are. A quality 6 volt battery is about $120-$130, and might last 3-4 years. A super capacitor should deliver far more cycles and be much more resistant to shock.

replies.author: @4gauge10
replies.text: @azurealism with a life expectancy over 40 years on top of that.

author: @Authoratah
text:
Is anyone else having trouble reaching the workingInk website?
The secure connection keeps giving me errors about my "correct time" !!!.....my browser has the correct time.
Something is wrong with the site's secure certificate

replies.author: @ealingbadger
replies.text: Same here - Firefox is complaining about a dodgy certificate.

replies.author: @scotttovey
replies.text:
You can try and refresh the network time on your computer.
Go into Control panel and click on "date and time".
Click the "Internet Time" tab and then click the "Change Settings" button.
Enter your password if prompted and then click the "Update now" button.
This will update your systems time to the most current time.
I had to do this once, otherwise, I would not suggest it as a possible solution to this problem.

replies.author: @Authoratah
replies.text:
@scotttovey it's not my phone, I checked my settings and my time is captured from the network and it is correct.
It is their website's certificate, it's expired and/or faulty

replies.author: @scotttovey
@ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: we are just sorting out the certificate mate - it should be fine tomorrow - I hope lol

author: @getyourbone
text: Can that split system above you head operate on dc...

replies.author: @garyhoffmann1615
replies.text: If it is an inverter AC they rectify the AC power coming in to about 340VDC then they invert the DC to the frequency it wants to control the compressor speed.
